author: Ram Rakha, L.
title: Nazara-i-Kashmir
gloss: A novel which describes the loves of Champawati and Tara Chand. Champawati was betrothed to a man whom she did not like, and who, being disappointed by the indifference of Champawati, committed suicide. She was afterwards married to Tara Chand.

author: Shadi Ram, Babu
title: Mysteries of Punjab
gloss: A story of two men, one a miser and the other wise. The latter spent money lavishly on the education of his children, who consequently became good and great, while the former kept his sons uneducated which ultimately involved the loss of all his property.
notes: Date given only as July 1910. English title on Urdu novel; education-as-uplift morality tale.

title: Yusuf wa Jamila
gloss: A domestic story in which the writer shows the evils of marriage in student life.
publisher: Malak Fazl-ud-din, Chanan Din and Taj-ud-din, Book-sellers
notes: Date given only as May 1910. End of URDU—FICTION (serials 23–29 this quarter).

author: Abdullah-ul-Imadi
title: Tarikh-i-Arab-i-Qadim
gloss: A brief history of the ancient Arabs.
notes: URDU—HISTORY serials continue from Q2. Same author-publisher pair as Falsafa-i-Ibn-i-Arabi (p25 s30).

author: Muhammad Abdur Razzaq, Munshi
title: Al-Baramikah
gloss: No. 18, volume I, of a series entitled the 'Wuzara-i-Islam', giving an account of the ministries of Yahya, Fazl and Ja'far, the three world-famed ministers of Caliph Harun-ur-Rashid, the illustrious monarch of the Abbaside dynasty of Baghdád, Parts I, II and III.
notes: Date given only as May 1910. Part of a numbered 'Ministers of Islam' series — serialized Islamic history publishing.

author: Ashraf, B. A., Mr.
title: Urdu Khush-khati
publisher: Author, who is Inspector of Mission Schools in Gurdáspur
notes: Section headed '(Designed for Educational purposes.)'. Date given only as May 1910. Author a mission-school inspector.
